Yes, you have described the process perfectly. When you multiply the denominator by its reciprocal, it equals 1, and multiplying the numerator by the same reciprocal keeps the value of the fraction unchanged. 

So to sum up, the average speed for the entire trip is 40 miles per hour because the total distance of $2d$ divided by the total time of $d/20$ simplifies mathematically to $2d \times (20/d)$, which equals 40. This rigorous application of the Multiplicative Inverse and Identity Properties proves why the average speed is 40 mph and not simply the average of the two speeds.
